rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ROBERT A. NAIFY LIVING TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 8, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: John Sherwoods (In His Individual Capacity Only) Notice Of General And Special Demurrers And General And Special Demurrers To Fourth Amended Petition For Relief
rulings.Rulings: John Sherwood's (in his individual capacity) General and Special Demurrer to Acela Cortese's Fourth Amended Petition is continued to October 10, 2018 at 2:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ROBERT A. NAIFY LIVING TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 8, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: General And Special Demurrers By Co-Trustees John Sherwood And Edward C. Topham, For The Robert A. Naify Living Trust, To Fourth Amended Petition For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Notice Of Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: The Co-Trustees' General and Special Demurrer to Acela Cortese's ("Acela") Fourth Amended Petition is sustained without leave to amend. Acela failed to timely file a creditor's claim under Probate Code 19100, or to avail herself of the late claims procedure set forth in Probate Code 19103, and cannot rectify these deficiencies even with additional leave to amend.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRANCESCA P. NAIFY LIVING TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 30, 1990

rulings.Rulings: John Sherwood's (in his individual capacity) General and Special Demurrer to Acela Cortese's Third Amended Petition is continued to October 10, 2018 at 2:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRANCESCA P. NAIFY LIVING TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 30, 1990
rulings.Calendar Matter: General And Special Demurrers By Co-Trustees John Sherwood And Edward C. Topham, For The Robert A. Naify Living Trust, To Third Amended Petition For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Notice Of Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Appearance is required regarding the Co-Trustees' General and Special Demurrer to Acela Cortese's Third Amended Petition. Acela filed her petition within the one-year statute of limitations set forth in CCP 366.2. It not clear how the creditor's claim procedure detailed in Probate Code 19000, et seq., and discussed at length in the papers, applies where no Notice to Creditors has been filed or published in the trust proceeding at issue, that is, the Francesca Naify Trust. The Co-Trustees' special demurrer to Acela's fraud claims is overruled as those claims are not "ambiguous or unintelligible" and thus are not sufficiently "uncertain" to warrant sustaining the special demurrer on that ground. However, the Co-Trustees' general demurrer to Acela's fraud claims (i.e., her fourth and fifth causes of action) is sustained without leave to amend. It is well established that fraud claims are subject to a stricter pleading standard. (See Glaski v. Bank of America, N.A. (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1079, 1090 [elements of fraud "may not be pleaded in a general or conclusory fashion."].) "'In an action for [common law] fraud, damage is an essential element of the cause of action.' ¿ "At the pleading stage, the complaint 'must show a cause and effect relationship between the fraud and damages sought; otherwise no cause of action is stated.'" (Small v. Fritz Companies, Inc. (2003) 30 Cal.4th 167, 202 [internal citations omitted].) Acela has failed to allege sufficient facts to show a causal connection between the alleged misrepresentations made by John and Robert and the harm she suffered, let alone the "complete causal relationship" required under applicable case law. (Ibid. ["A 'complete causal relationship' between the fraud or deceit and the plaintiff's damages is required."].) The facts alleged regarding causal damages are insufficient, despite this being the third iteration of Acela's petition, and Acela has made no attempt to set forth "additional facts to be pleaded" showing "that there is a reasonable possibility the defect can be cured to state a cause of action." (CCP 430.41(e)(1).) This being the third amendment to Acela's petition - and all amendments having been made after the filing of demurrer(s) - the Court finds it inappropriate to grant additional leave to amend at this juncture.
